target.title: feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
target.body:
# PR 2 draft: Feishu reaction tool registration

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_reaction` tool with `add`, `remove`, and `list` actions.
- Wire the tool through Feishu tool config defaults, config validation, account-level tool merging, plugin metadata, API exports, and bundled entry registration.
- Normalize common emoji aliases such as `👍`, `heart`, `clap`, and `ok` to Feishu emoji type strings before calling the reactions API.
- Preserve canonical Feishu mixed-case emoji type strings such as `Typing` instead of uppercasing unknown values.
- Honor the existing `actions.reactions` policy gate as well as the new `tools.reactions` tool-registration gate.
- Document the Feishu-native reaction tool and `channels.feishu.tools.reactions` gate.

## Related upstream work

- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/39567
- Addresses the missing reaction-tool portion of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8692
- Complements the lower-level reaction bug fixes in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66406 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478
- Keeps scope narrow and does not change the existing generic `message(action="react")` path, reducing overlap with https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/34528 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Notes for reviewers

- This PR registers the already-existing Feishu reactions API surface as an agent tool.
- `tools.reactions` defaults to `false`; the native tool is opt-in because generic `message(action="react")` already covers Feishu reactions.
- `actions.reactions: false` disables both generic reaction actions and this native tool path.
- `remove` accepts either a Feishu `reaction_id` or an `emoji_type`; when an emoji type is provided, the tool removes the bot/app-owned matching reaction.
- Native and generic reaction removal now share ownership-safe helpers; emoji normalization for the native tool preserves unknown/canonical Feishu values.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can use the native `feishu_reaction` tool to add, list, and remove emoji reactions on real Feishu messages instead of having the reactions API implemented but unreachable from agents.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scope `im:message:reactions:write_only` enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Loaded the PR branch locally.
2. Read the existing Feishu bot config from `C:\Users\Clark\.openclaw\openclaw.json` without printing secrets.
3. Enabled `channels.feishu.tools.reactions: true` and `channels.feishu.actions.reactions: true` in memory for the live run.
4. Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` to import `extensions/feishu/src/reaction-tool.ts` from this branch, call `registerFeishuReactionTools(...)`, resolve the registered `feishu_reaction` tool, and execute `tool.execute(...)` against the live Feishu API.
5. Used the latest real Feishu `message_id` from the local gateway dedup state as the target.
6. Executed `remove OK` pre-clean, then `add OK`, `list OK`, `remove OK`, and final `list OK`.

Evidence after fix:
Copied terminal output from the live command. IDs are redacted; no app secret/token is included.

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "feishu_reaction add/list/remove live API proof",
  "source": "local OpenClaw PR branch tool.execute against Feishu API",
  "selectedMessage": {
    "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
    "dedupSeenAtMs": 1779090392068
  },
  "account": {
    "domain": "feishu",
    "appId": "cli_aa...2dcce",
    "toolsReactions": true,
    "actionsReactions": true
  },
  "assertions": {
    "ownAfterAdd": 1,
    "ownAfterRemove": 0
  },
  "transcript": [
    {
      "label": "pre-clean remove OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "remove",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "removed": false
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "add OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "add",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "reaction_id": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx"
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "list OK after add",
      "params": {
        "action": "list",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "reactions": [
          {
            "reactionId": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx",
            "emojiType": "OK",
            "operatorType": "app",
            "operatorId": "cli_aa...2dcce"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "remove OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "remove",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "removed": {
          "reaction_id": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "list OK after remove",
      "params": {
        "action": "list",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "reactions": []
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: `add` returned a live `reaction_id`; `list` immediately returned one app-owned `OK` reaction whose `operatorId` matched the redacted app id; `remove` deleted that reaction; the final `list` returned an empty reaction list for `OK`.

What was not tested: visual screen recording of the Feishu chat UI; the proof above is copied terminal output from the real Feishu bot environment.

## Testing

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/reaction-tool.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tools-config.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/docx.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts
# 5 files, 73 tests passed

.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 778 tests passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

.\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check ...
# passed

.\node_modules\.bin\oxlint.cmd ...
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```

target.title: feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall
target.body:
# PR 1 draft: Feishu agent message delete and recall

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall actions
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_message` tool for `list`, `delete`, `recall`, `read_receipts`, and `read_users`.
- Wire Feishu `messages` tool config through defaults, schema validation, plugin metadata, API exports, and runtime registration.
- Expose Telegram-like generic message actions for Feishu: `delete` and `unsend`.
- Track the latest bot-sent Feishu message per account/chat so natural-language requests like "delete your previous message" can work even when the model omits `messageId`.
- Document Feishu message-management permissions, Unix-second time filters, and delete/recall behavior.

## Related upstream work

- Closes / revives https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/51422
- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/52993
- Complements, but does not replace, inbound recall-event work in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/55225 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/55691
- Leaves Feishu reaction tool registration for a follow-up PR, because reaction fixes are already active in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Fixes compared with #52993

- `start_time` and `end_time` are documented and validated as Unix-second timestamp strings, matching Feishu's API, instead of ISO 8601 strings.
- `delete` and `recall` have distinct request paths and user-facing messages; delete no longer reuses recall semantics such as "cannot recall after two minutes".
- Missing Feishu scopes are surfaced with actionable messages, including `im:message.group_msg` for group-message deletion.

## Notes for reviewers

- Feishu delete behaves as a platform soft-delete/recall: follow-up reads can return `deleted: true`, `updated: true`, and empty content. Some clients may still show a deletion placeholder.
- The last-bot-message fallback is intentionally in-memory and scoped by account/chat with a 24-hour TTL. It only records messages sent by this bot process.
- The feature is gated by `tools.messages`, defaulting to `false` because message list/delete/recall/read-receipts are sensitive.

## Testing

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/message-tool.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts
# 4 files, 104 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts
# 2 files, 84 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 786 tests passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxlint extensions/feishu/src/sent-message-cache.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can delete or recall bot messages from a real group chat through the new `feishu_message` tool and generic `message(action="delete"|"unsend")` path.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw gateway from this branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scopes including `im:message:update`, `im:message:readonly`, and `im:message.group_msg`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enabled the opt-in message management gate for the Feishu test account, started the local gateway from this branch with `openclaw gateway run --force`, sent messages in the real Feishu group, first verified delete failed before `im:message.group_msg` was granted, granted the scope and restarted the gateway, sent a live bot reply, asked the bot to delete its previous message, then checked the deleted message through Feishu message read output.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output and redacted runtime notes from the real Feishu setup:

```text
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Feishu WebSocket ready at 2026-05-18T15:43:04+08:00

Initial group delete attempt:
  Feishu API error code=230027
  msg="need scope: im:message.group_msg"
  log_id=20260518150333B37818A84E685EE4FE60

After granting im:message.group_msg:
  sent message_id=om_x100b6f9fbdc2c8e4c3bb0f1440e9c1d
  im.message.delete response={"code":0,"msg":"success"}
  follow-up message read:
    deleted=true
    updated=true
    content_length=0

Natural-language group flow after last-bot-message fallback:
  user: delete your previous message
  agent called message(action="delete") without messageId
  fallback resolved last bot message in chat oc_* to om_*
  Feishu delete returned deleted=true
```

Observed result after fix: The bot replied in the Feishu group, deleted its own previous message after the required group-message scope was enabled, and Feishu readback showed the target message was deleted with empty content. The natural-language "delete your previous message" case also worked without the model supplying a `messageId`.

What was not tested: No additional gaps

target.title: fix(control-ui): project message tool sends as chat bubbles
target.body:
## Summary
- Problem: Control UI history renders visible replies sent through `message(action="send")` only as tool call/tool result blocks.

- What changed: successful `message(action="send")` tool calls are projected into a synthetic assistant text bubble while the original tool blocks remain available for debugging.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): message delivery, channel routing, tool execution, and failed `message` sends are unchanged.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: bundled the patched `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts` with `node_modules/.bin/esbuild`, then ran Node against a redacted successful `message(action="send")` assistant tool call plus matching successful `toolName: "message"` result.

target.title: fix(minimax): normalize OAuth token expiry to absolute millisecond timestamp
target.body:
## Summary

Converts MiniMax OAuth token `expired_in` from relative seconds to an absolute millisecond timestamp, fixing `hasUsableOAuthCredential()` always treating the token as expired.

This PR narrows the fix to the MiniMax Portal OAuth expiry path. It does not claim to fix the separate MiniMax API-key path discussed on #83449.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: MiniMax Portal OAuth credentials in OpenClaw should stop looking immediately expired, so requests no longer spend 30-50s repeatedly stuck in auth before reaching the MiniMax provider.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this branch on macOS, using the existing `minimax-portal` OAuth profile from `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `pnpm openclaw models status`
2. `TIMEFMT='real %E'; time pnpm openclaw infer model run --local --model minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7 --prompt 'Reply with OK only.' --json`
3. Re-ran step 2 once more without changing auth state.

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture after this patch:

```text
Auth overview
- minimax-portal effective=profiles:~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json | profiles=1 (oauth=1, token=0, api_key=0) | minimax-portal:default=OAuth

OAuth/token status
- minimax-portal usage: 5h 0% left
  - minimax-portal:default ok expires in 364d

Error: No text output returned for provider "minimax-portal" model "MiniMax-M2.7": 400 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"invalid params, model[MiniMax-M2.7] does not support max tokens > 196608 (2013)"},"request_id":"[redacted]"}.
real 5.78s

Error: No text output returned for provider "minimax-portal" model "MiniMax-M2.7": 400 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"invalid params, model[MiniMax-M2.7] does not support max tokens > 196608 (2013)"},"request_id":"[redacted]"}.
real 6.18s
```

Observed result after fix: The stored MiniMax Portal OAuth credential was recognized as valid (`ok expires in 364d`), and two consecutive real local `minimax-portal` requests reached the provider and returned the same provider-side 400 in about 6 seconds instead of reproducing the original 30-50s auth-stage stall.

What was not tested: Successful MiniMax text generation beyond this provider-side 400 response, the separate MiniMax API-key path, and the installed 2026.5.12 gateway binary outside this local branch run.

## Root Cause

MiniMax returns `expired_in` from the token endpoint as a relative duration in seconds (standard OAuth `expires_in` semantics), but the auth profile store's [`hasUsableOAuthCredential()`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/agents/auth-profiles/credential-state.ts#L42) expects an absolute millisecond timestamp:

```
// credential-state.ts: remainingMs = expires - Date.now()
// 86400 - 1716000000000 < 0 → always "expired"
```

Without conversion the token appears perpetually expired, triggering a slow OAuth refresh network call to `api.minimaxi.com` on **every request**. Combined with CN network latency this causes the reported 30-50s auth-stage delay.

## Changes

**`extensions/minimax/oauth.ts`**: Added `normalizeOAuthExpires()` that handles three possible `expired_in` formats:
- Relative duration in seconds (< 1B): `Date.now() + expiredIn * 1000`
- Absolute Unix timestamp in seconds (1B–1T): `expiredIn * 1000`
- Absolute millisecond timestamp (>= 1T): use as-is

**`extensions/minimax/oauth.test.ts`**: Added focused regression coverage for relative seconds, Unix seconds, and millisecond timestamps.

## Verification

- [x] MiniMax OAuth expiry normalization tests: 3 pass
- [x] MiniMax provider extension tests: 9 pass
- [x] MiniMax extension tests: 107 pass
- [x] Auth controller tests: 7 pass
- [x] Auth profile tests: 70 pass
- [x] MiniMax contract tests: 7 pass
- [x] Build succeeds
- [x] Formatting clean

Related #83449.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve raw external session aliases
target.body:
Closes #63005.

## Behavior addressed

External raw session keys such as `conversation:pair:...` and opaque provider keys such as `Signal:Group:<mixed-case-id>` should survive gateway session-key canonicalization. Any follow-up mutation should keep the raw key and the visible `agent:<id>:` canonical key pointing at the same session entry instead of splitting history or plugin-owned state.

## Root cause

`migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` canonicalized raw external keys before preserving the raw-key store entry as an alias of the canonical agent key. The first fix preserved that raw alias, but some write paths still replaced, mutated, or deleted only one key.

The later review findings exposed the remaining gaps:

- checkpoint restore, checkpoint metadata, manual compaction, and maxLines compaction metadata paths still wrote only the canonical entry
- alias discovery lowercased opaque Signal group peer IDs
- after `sessions.list` hid the raw alias, canonical-key patch/delete/reset/compact flows did not rediscover the hidden raw alias
- plugin host-hook writers (`enqueuePluginNextTurnInjection`, `drainPluginNextTurnInjections`, and `patchPluginSessionExtension`) updated only `loaded.storeKey`, leaving hidden raw aliases with stale plugin state

The canonical-key derivation also has to preserve separators such as `user_a::user_b`, so it cannot use `parseAgentSessionKey` for raw-rest extraction because that parser drops empty segments.

## Fix

The preserved raw-alias logic is now shared in `src/gateway/session-store-aliases.ts`, with alias-aware write, sync, and delete helpers.

Covered mutation paths now route writes through the shared alias contract:

- `sessions.patch`
- `sessions.delete`
- `sessions.reset`
- agent writeback
- node-event store touches
- resolver sync
- `sessions.compaction.restore`
- compaction checkpoint metadata persistence
- `sessions.compact` manual metadata updates
- `sessions.compact` maxLines metadata updates
- plugin next-turn injection enqueue/drain
- plugin session-extension patch/unset

Preserved raw aliases stay internal-only for lookup continuity: `sessions.list` filtering/search/count collapses a raw+canonical alias pair into one visible canonical session row.

Canonical visible keys now rediscover an existing hidden raw alias by stripping the `agent:<id>:` prefix while preserving the rest of the key exactly. Canonical-only sessions do not grow a new raw alias.

Opaque Signal group peer IDs are preserved when deriving raw aliases, so mixed-case group IDs do not create lowercased duplicate logical session rows.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Raw external session aliases are preserved during canonicalization, stay synchronized across direct session mutations, compaction/checkpoint lifecycle writes, canonical visible-key follow-ups, and plugin host-hook writes, while keeping only one visible canonical row in list/search/count.

**Real environment tested:** Source checkout on PR head `1cde6d4a5d222273ed4c9c69b5d6e4ac402282c9`, base `f07c87405c3094feceaaf2f0f19a2e765a25e78d`, Node 22, repo dependencies installed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node --import tsx /private/tmp/pr83368-proof.ts
```

Also ran the targeted and broader regression commands listed in the Validation section below.

**Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
  "preservedAliasKeys": [
    "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
  ],
  "afterWrite": {
    "canonicalSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSessionId": "sess-write",
    "rawSendPolicy": "deny",
    "sameEntry": true
  },
  "visible": {
    "listKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "listCount": 1,
    "listTotalCount": 1,
    "searchKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "searchCount": 1,
    "searchTotalCount": 1
  },
  "deleted": true,
  "afterDelete": {
    "canonicalSessionId": null,
    "rawSessionId": null
  },
  "canonicalVisibleKeyFlow": {
    "listedKeys": [
      "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
    ],
    "afterWrite": {
      "visibleKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
      "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
      "preservedAliasKeys": [
        "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
      ],
      "canonicalSessionId": "sess-visible-write",
      "rawSessionId": "sess-visible-write",
      "rawSendPolicy": "deny",
      "sameEntry": true
    },
    "deleted": true,
    "afterDelete": {
      "canonicalSessionId": null,
      "rawSessionId": null
    }
  }
}
```

The command printed local stale-plugin config warnings for `multica`; they are unrelated to this PR and did not affect the session-store proof.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw alias and canonical key share the same written entry after replacement, including patched `sendPolicy`; list and search return one visible canonical row with `totalCount: 1`; delete removes both keys. Starting from the visible canonical list key also rediscovers the hidden raw alias, writes both aliases, and deletes both aliases. The plugin regressions show canonical-key plugin next-turn enqueue/drain and session-extension patch/unset update or clear the hidden raw alias too.

**What was not tested:** No full live external chat-channel round trip was run. No screenshots are attached because this is backend session-store behavior with no visual UI surface.

## Regression Coverage

- `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` covers raw/canonical alias collapse, mixed-case Signal opaque-ID preservation, canonical-key rediscovery, and canonical-only no-new-raw-alias behavior.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts` covers list-to-patch/list-to-delete using the visible canonical key while the hidden raw alias follows.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts` covers reset alias synchronization.
- `src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts` covers checkpoint metadata persistence to both aliases.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` covers checkpoint restore, manual compaction metadata, and maxLines compaction metadata alias sync.
- `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` covers canonical-key plugin next-turn injection enqueue and drain syncing the hidden raw alias.
- `src/plugins/contracts/session-entry-projection.contract.test.ts` covers canonical-key plugin session-extension patch and unset syncing the hidden raw alias and promoted slot.

## Validation

Ran on head `1cde6d4a5d222273ed4c9c69b5d6e4ac402282c9`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-entry-projection.contract.test.ts
Test Files  6 passed (6)
Tests       347 passed (347)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-entry-projection.contract.test.ts
Test Files  17 passed (17)
Tests       650 passed (650)
```

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/session-store-aliases.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/plugins/host-hook-state.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

```text
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/session-store-aliases.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/plugins/host-hook-state.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-entry-projection.contract.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```


## Screenshots

No screenshots apply because this is backend session-store alias synchronization, not a visual UI surface.

target.title: fix(telegram): prevent polling stalls via TCP keepalive and self-flagging guard
target.body:
## Summary

Three complementary fixes for Telegram polling stalls observed in production where the long-poll `getUpdates` connection silently dies and the gateway either hangs until the stall watchdog fires (~90s) or self-flags `disconnected` during a healthy poll-cycle restart.

### 1. Lower undici TCP keepalive initial delay to 30s (`extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts`)

**Background:** undici 8.3.0's connector already enables TCP keepalive by default with a 60s initial delay (`socket.setKeepAlive(true, 60000)`). The fix here is not "enable keepalive from off" — it is tightening the timing.

**Root cause:** Long-poll connections to `api.telegram.org` stay idle for up to the `getUpdates` timeout (~900s). Most home/office NAT tables expire idle TCP entries after 60-1800s (commonly ~60-120s for SOHO routers). undici's default 60s initial delay leaves no headroom: a NAT that expires at 60s drops the entry before the first keepalive probe fires. When the NAT entry is silently dropped the TCP connection hangs rather than returning an error, leaving the grammY runner stuck until the 90s stall watchdog fires and forces a restart cycle.

**Fix:** Make the connect options explicit and lower `keepAliveInitialDelay` to 30s on the undici Agent `connect` options built in `buildTelegramConnectOptions`:

```ts
const TELEGRAM_KEEPALIVE_INITIAL_DELAY_MS = 30_000;
return {
  keepAlive: true,
  keepAliveInitialDelay: TELEGRAM_KEEPALIVE_INITIAL_DELAY_MS,
  // ...
};
```

OS-level TCP keepalive probes now start firing at 30s instead of 60s, which:
1. Refreshes the NAT table entry before short-window NATs (60s) expire it.
2. Surfaces dead connections faster with `ETIMEDOUT` instead of waiting through undici's default 60s warmup.

The explicit form also documents the dependency contract so future undici changes don't silently change the timing.

This is the transport-layer prevention complement to upstream's existing detection-layer work (#78646 watchdog, #81746 event-loop stall detection, #82256 spool recovery, #82227 outbound drain). Detection mechanisms still catch any failure mode that escapes the keepalive; this PR cuts the volume of stalls that those mechanisms have to handle.

### 2. Clear inherited `connected` state on polling start; widen channel connect grace to 300s

**Root cause:**
- `notePollingStart` was unconditionally setting `connected: false` at the start of every poll cycle, causing the downstream health monitor to see an apparent disconnect during legitimate reconnect cycles and trigger unnecessary restarts.
- `DEFAULT_CHANNEL_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` was 120s. Slow-startup channels (e.g. bots with many commands, large first-page payloads) finish the full handshake after 2-3 minutes. The health monitor evaluated them as `disconnected` before they finished connecting, causing premature restart cycles.

**Fix:**
- `polling-status.ts`: `notePollingStart` resets all polling lifecycle telemetry — including writing `connected: false` so a fresh lifecycle is never inheriting a stale `connected: true` from the previous runtime patch-merge. This is the right architectural shape because runtime status in `server-channels.ts` is patch-merged: a startup that omits `connected` would inherit the previous lifecycle's flag.
- `channel-health-policy.ts`: `DEFAULT_CHANNEL_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` widened from 120_000 to 300_000. This is in core (shared across channels) because the underlying property — long handshake time before stable connection — is not telegram-specific. Any channel with a slow first connect benefits. The Telegram status diagnostic (`status-issues.ts`) aligns `TELEGRAM_POLLING_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` to the same 300s constant so `openclaw channels status` and the background health monitor agree on the suppression window.

### 3. Defense-in-depth `evaluateChannelHealth` branch for inherited `connected: true`

**Why it's here:** With fix #2 in place, a hung polling startup is normally caught by `evaluateChannelHealth`'s existing `connected === false` branch after the 300s grace. But the patch-merge architecture means a future code path (a new lifecycle hook, a refactor that re-orders the patch sequence) could silently regress by forgetting to clear `connected`. This branch defends against that case.

**Fix:** Post-grace branch in `evaluateChannelHealth` that flags polling channels as `stale-socket` when `connected: true` is paired with null `lastConnectedAt` and null `lastTransportActivityAt` and a non-null `lastStartAt`. Scoped to `mode === "polling"` so webhook channels and channels without continuous transport tracking are not falsely flagged.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified explicit keepalive args activate: `keepAlive: true` and `keepAliveInitialDelay: 30_000` confirmed in undici Agent connect options via existing `polling-fetch.test.ts` assertions on the Agent config.
- [x] `polling-status.test.ts` updated: covers the new `notePollingStart`-writes-connected-false behavior and the cycle-start telemetry reset.
- [x] `polling-session.test.ts` updated: covers the resulting disconnected-patch shape during recoverable restart cycles.
- [x] `channel-health-policy.test.ts`: 3 new test cases covering the defensive branch (flagged path, in-grace happy path, prior-successful-connect happy path) plus an explicit inherited-connected-true case.
- [x] `status.test.ts`: post-grace spool-handler and polling-startup tests aligned with the widened 300s `TELEGRAM_POLLING_CONNECT_GRACE_MS`.
- [x] Local acceptance: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-status.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/status.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts src/gateway/channel-health-policy.test.ts` — 173 tests pass.
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` exit 0.
- [x] Verified stable in production on two hosts (mac-mini, rh-bot) over 3+ days with zero telegram polling stalls.

## Diagnosis signature this PR resolves

`~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` repeated entries of the form:

```
[telegram] [diag] polling cycle error reason=getUpdates ... err=Call to 'getUpdates' failed! ... timeout
[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 10000ms ... operation=fetchWithTimeout url=https://api.telegram.org/.../getMe
```

paired with `gateway-health-monitor` restarting the telegram channel due to a "disconnected" reading shortly after a normal poll-cycle start.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram long-poll `getUpdates` connection hangs silently after NAT-table entry expires; gateway either waits ~90s for the in-process stall watchdog or self-flags `disconnected` during a legitimate poll-cycle restart, triggering unnecessary channel restart cycles.
- **Real environment tested**: 2 production gateway hosts running this branch as part of `upgrade-2026.5.12` (which carries the original transport + lifecycle commits): `mac-mini.lan` (Kbot / @Kebablebot + @RattyMcRatfaceBot) and `rh-bot.lan` (Requesty / @requestymcrequestfacebot). macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 22+, undici via grammY runner long-poll.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On each host:
  openclaw gateway status            # confirm runtime
  openclaw channels status           # confirm telegram polling status + last-activity timestamps
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (live terminal output, mac-mini.lan, 2026-05-18)**:
  ```
  CLI version: 2026.5.12 (/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw)
  Gateway version: 2026.5.12
  Runtime: running (pid 50650, state active)
  Connectivity probe: ok
  - Telegram default: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:12h ago, out:16m ago, mode:polling, token:config, groups:unmentioned
  - Telegram ratbot: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:28h ago, out:28h ago, mode:polling, token:config, groups:unmentioned
  ```
  Same gateway pid (50650) has now held for over 24h continuously, with telegram outbound activity within the last 16 minutes for the default account. Pre-fix observation log signature `[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 10000ms ... operation=fetchWithTimeout url=https://api.telegram.org/...` no longer appears in `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` post-fix on either host.
- **Observed result after fix**: Both gateways' telegram channels report `running, connected` continuously since deploy; no `[telegram] [diag] polling cycle error reason=getUpdates ... err=...timeout` repeats; no health-monitor-initiated channel restarts; runtime uptime measured in days not minutes.
- **What was not tested**: Behavior under deliberately-induced NAT entry expiry (would need a NAT/router scenario I can't reproduce on the LAN). Behavior under a host with TCP keepalive disabled at the OS level — undici docs imply the connector's `setKeepAlive(true, ...)` call enables it regardless, but I haven't verified that path explicitly. Final-head live proof on the merged `faaa9e77deda` snapshot (production hosts are still on the original transport+lifecycle subset).

target.title: fix(heartbeat): rename target-none skip reason to delivery-disabled
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: The default heartbeat target is `none`, which is documented to mean "the heartbeat runs but does not deliver externally". When that path is taken, the skipped heartbeat event reports `reason: "target-none"`, which operators consistently misread as "no recipient/session was resolved" and start debugging channels, presence, allowlists, and session routing — none of which are involved.
- **Why it matters**: misleading diagnostic wording costs operator time during incident triage and obscures correct, by-design system behavior. Issue #44534 documents the misread explicitly.
- **What changed**: renamed the skip reason from `target-none` to `delivery-disabled` in `src/infra/outbound/targets.ts`, and updated the matching expectations in `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: no changes to heartbeat scheduling, delivery resolution, target priority, defaults, or configuration handling. Pure diagnostic-string rename. Other reasons emitted by the same `buildNoHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` helper (`unknown-account`, `no-target`, `dm-blocked`) are intentionally untouched.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #44534
- Supersedes #46870, which was closed only because the author hit the 20-open-PR cap (ClawSweeper's keep-open review confirmed the rename shape was acceptable on technical grounds).
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: heartbeat skip events and `openclaw system heartbeat last` now report `reason: "delivery-disabled"` for the default-unset and explicit `target: "none"` configs, instead of the misleading `target-none`.
- **Real environment tested**: local source checkout on Windows 11 (Node 24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0). Ran a Node + tsx script that imports `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` from the patched source and exercises both branches users actually hit, plus the remembered-`lastChannel` variant.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: from repo root, ran `node --import tsx ./proof.mjs` where `proof.mjs` imports `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` from `src/infra/outbound/targets.ts` and prints the result for three configs: (a) `{}`, (b) `{ agents: { defaults: { heartbeat: { target: "none" } } } }`, (c) the same as (b) plus a remembered `lastChannel: "whatsapp"`.
- **Evidence after fix**: live stdout copied from `node --import tsx ./proof.mjs`; no model invocation involved.
  ```text
  # default heartbeat (unset target — most users)
  {
    "channel": "none",
    "reason": "delivery-disabled"
  }

  # explicit target=none
  {
    "channel": "none",
    "reason": "delivery-disabled"
  }

  # explicit target=none with a remembered last channel
  {
    "channel": "none",
    "reason": "delivery-disabled",
    "lastChannel": "whatsapp"
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: the misleading `target-none` reason is gone; all three branches that previously emitted it now emit the operator-meaningful `delivery-disabled`. Remembered `lastChannel` is still threaded through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: full live gateway service startup + `openclaw system heartbeat last` CLI presentation against an installed gateway with an enabled channel. Not required for a string rename — `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` is the single emission site for this reason, and the unit tests cover the call shape `openclaw system heartbeat last` consumes.
- **Before evidence**: documented in the issue body of #44534 (operator hit the misleading `target-none` reason in the wild on 2026.4.x).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: the skip reason string was named after the implementation detail (`target` value is `none`) rather than the operator-facing meaning (delivery is intentionally disabled). When verbose diagnostics surface the raw reason, the wording invites the wrong mental model.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: existing tests pinned the literal `target-none` value, which prevented the diagnostic wording from drifting toward something more accurate.
- **Contributing context**: the `none` value flows from the documented default at `agents.defaults.heartbeat.target` (`docs/gateway/heartbeat.md`). The docs already say `none` means the heartbeat runs but does not deliver externally — the diagnostic just didn't match that documented intent.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` (cases "target none", "target defaults to none when unset", and "skip webchat last route").
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: skip reason for both default-unset target and explicit `target: "none"` is `delivery-disabled` (and not `target-none`), in both the no-remembered-channel and remembered-channel branches.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: the reason value is a deterministic single-emission output of `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget`. The three pinned cases lock in the value across the configurations users actually hit.
- **Existing test that already covers this**: the same three cases already existed and pinned the old `target-none` value; this PR updates them to the new value.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw system heartbeat last` (and any consumer of the skipped heartbeat event) now reports:

```json
{ "status": "skipped", "reason": "delivery-disabled" }
```

instead of the older `reason: "target-none"`. The skip behavior itself is unchanged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: Windows 11
- Runtime: Node 24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A (heartbeat delivery target resolution; no model invoked)
- Integration/channel: heartbeat default config (target unset → resolves to `none`)
- Relevant config: synthetic in-memory `OpenClawConfig` snapshots covering default-unset and explicit `target: "none"`

### Steps
1. Apply this patch.
2. From repo root, run a tiny Node + tsx script that imports `resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` from `src/infra/outbound/targets.ts` and calls it with: (a) `{}`, (b) `{ agents: { defaults: { heartbeat: { target: "none" } } } }`, (c) the same as (b) plus a remembered `lastChannel`.
3. Inspect the returned `reason`.

### Expected
- `reason: "delivery-disabled"` in all three cases.

### Actual
- `reason: "delivery-disabled"` in all three cases (see live output under Real behavior proof above).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — the three pinned expectations in `heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` would have failed against this rename without their corresponding update; both are bundled in the same commit.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — see Real behavior proof.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording — N/A (single string rename; no UI surface change).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A.

Targeted Vitest runs after the patch:

```text
$ pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  36 passed (36)
   Duration  5.15s

$ pnpm test src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets-loaded.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  71 passed (71)
   Duration  7.21s
```

Formatting + lint:

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/outbound/targets.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm check:changed
# exit 0 (no findings)

$ git diff --check
# clean
```

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: default-unset heartbeat config, explicit `target: "none"` config, and the explicit-none + remembered-`lastChannel` variant. All three return `reason: "delivery-disabled"`. Confirmed via a live `node --import tsx` import of the patched source.
- **Edge cases checked**: only the one user-visible reason string changes. The other four `buildNoHeartbeatDeliveryTarget` call sites in `targets.ts` emit different reasons (`unknown-account`, `no-target`, `dm-blocked`) and were intentionally left untouched. Verified that the QA scenario file `qa/scenarios/memory/commitments-heartbeat-target-none.md` and the `stableSchedulerSeed`/temp-dir labels in `heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts` use `target-none` only as a fixture name (not the user-visible reason) and do not need to change.
- **What I did NOT verify**: live gateway service startup + CLI presentation against a running gateway, and any external scripts that may grep for the literal `target-none` reason string. The diagnostic is informational; rotating its value is exactly what the linked issue and ClawSweeper triage recommend.

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Behavior: yes. The reason string is informational, not part of any documented contract. Scripts that key on the literal `target-none` would need to read `delivery-disabled` — and that rename is exactly what #44534 asks for.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert: revert this single commit. There is no migration state to roll back.

## Risks and Mitigations

None identified for the rename itself. The closed predecessor #46870 used the same rename target and was not rejected on technical grounds.

## AI-assisted

This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the diff, ran the targeted Vitest lanes, oxfmt check, check:changed, and real-behavior proof locally, and confirm the output above is from my machine.

target.title: fix(agents): guard stale session lock removal
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #57019.

Session write-lock stale recovery now uses `@openclaw/fs-safe`'s snapshot-safe `staleRecovery: "remove-if-unchanged"` path instead of re-reading a reported stale lock and deleting it directly by path in OpenClaw's wrapper.

## Problem summary

When `SESSION_LOCKS.acquire()` reported `file_lock_stale`, `acquireSessionWriteLock()` re-read the lock file and called `fs.rm(lockPath, { force: true })` itself before retrying. That second path was not tied to the exact sidecar snapshot that fs-safe had observed, so a lock file that changed between stale detection and cleanup could still be removed by path.

## Root cause

The session-lock wrapper duplicated stale cleanup outside the file-lock dependency. `@openclaw/fs-safe` already has a remove-if-unchanged recovery mode that re-reads the sidecar and compares the raw content/file identity before unlinking; OpenClaw was not opting into that contract and instead performed a path-only removal after `file_lock_stale`.

## Architectural reasoning

The stale-owner policy remains in `src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`: dead PID, recycled PID, non-OpenClaw owner, orphan self-PID, payloadless, and mtime fallback decisions still use the existing `inspectLockPayloadForSession()` and `shouldReclaimContendedLockFile()` logic.

The actual deletion now belongs to the lower-level lock manager, which is the component that owns the observed sidecar snapshot. This avoids a hidden coupling between stale classification and path deletion while preserving the existing retry/acquire behavior for genuinely stale locks.

## Behavior proof

Behavior addressed: session lock stale recovery no longer deletes a lock file if the file changes after stale classification but before removal.

Real environment tested: local Windows checkout on branch `Hemant/session-lock-stale-snapshot`, Node v22.15.0 (below the repo's declared >=22.16.0 engine; commands completed with the existing engine warning). The branch was rebased onto upstream/main `73ca3cf3c3dd3940b82f4680e6a38fc36e272d81`; upstream moved again while checks ran, and GitHub reported the PR mergeable after push.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
pnpm test src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check -- src/agents/session-write-lock.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD
rtk node --import tsx --input-type=module -e "<one-off script invoking production acquireSessionWriteLock with a deterministic stale-removal race hook>"
```

Evidence after fix: `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts` passed 35/35 tests, including the regression `does not remove a changed lock file during stale acquisition recovery`; format check passed for the two touched files; `pnpm check:changed` exited 0; `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` exited 0. GitHub Workflow Sanity, OpenGrep, CI, CodeQL, and CodeQL Critical Quality all passed on the refreshed head.

Additional terminal behavior proof:

```json
{
  "behavior": "changed stale lock preserved during recovery",
  "hookCalls": 1,
  "finalRaw": "{}",
  "lockPreserved": true,
  "acquired": false,
  "errorName": "SessionWriteLockTimeoutError",
  "errorMessage": "session file locked (timeout 75ms): unknown <temp>/sessions.json.lock"
}
```

Observed result after fix: stale recovery still reclaims the existing stale lock cases, but both the focused regression and terminal proof mutate the lock during the approved removal window and verify acquisition fails closed with the changed lock file still present instead of unlinking it.

What was not tested: full `pnpm test` was not run locally. I relied on the focused session-lock test plus `check:changed`; CI completed successfully on this PR.

## Testing performed

Local proof on refreshed head `d21c47374092b4b4ee7d2afc810fb4ffed661123`:

- `pnpm test src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed: 35/35.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/session-write-lock.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- `rtk node --import tsx --input-type=module -e "<one-off script invoking production acquireSessionWriteLock with a deterministic stale-removal race hook>"`

GitHub Actions on `d21c47374092b4b4ee7d2afc810fb4ffed661123`:

- Workflow Sanity #201593: success
- OpenGrep PR Diff #21215: success
- CI #207307: success
- CodeQL #17038: success
- CodeQL Critical Quality #17171: success

## Risk analysis

Risk is low-to-medium because this touches session lock cleanup and contention handling. The change is narrow: stale classification logic is unchanged, lock release logic is unchanged, and the deletion operation moves to fs-safe's existing snapshot guard. The main behavioral difference is fail-closed behavior if the lock file changes during stale recovery, which is the intended safety property for mutual exclusion.

## Backward compatibility

No public API, config, gateway protocol, or session file format changes. Existing stale lock recovery semantics are preserved for unchanged stale lock files; changed files are now protected from path-only deletion.

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

target.title: fix(cli-runner): keep recent tail when reseed history exceeds maxHistoryChars
target.body:
## Summary

`buildCliSessionHistoryPrompt` in `src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.ts` prefix-slices the rendered transcript when it exceeds `maxHistoryChars`, so when a Claude-CLI session reseeds with a long history, the **most recent turns get dropped** and the agent acts as if its own latest reply (and the user's latest ask) never happened. Universal post-#80934, since that PR turned on the reseed path by default for Claude CLI in `2026.5.12`.

Closes #83157.

This PR flips the slice direction (`slice(0, n)` → `slice(-n)`), moves the truncation marker to the lead so it correctly describes what follows (older turns dropped, recent tail retained), updates the existing cap-test marker assertion, and adds regression tests that the LAST message in `params.messages` survives in the rendered prompt when the input exceeds the cap. The structure-aware truncation also pins a leading `compactionSummary` entry as a prefix and only tail-truncates the post-summary transcript, so the compacted prior context survives reseed even when the post-summary tail alone exceeds the cap.

- Problem: reseed prompt drops the latest turn instead of the oldest when rendered history > 12288 chars.
- Why it matters: every Claude-CLI user hits this whenever a `session_expired` or other reseed-eligible reason fires mid-conversation; the recovered session immediately loses the most recent context.
- What changed: `slice(0, maxHistoryChars).trimEnd()` → `slice(-maxHistoryChars).trimStart()`, plus marker phrasing/position update, structure-aware truncation that pins a leading `compactionSummary` as a prefix and head-slices the summary itself when it alone exceeds the cap, and three regression tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the 12288-char cap itself is intentionally not touched (separate config-knob discussion).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage (CLI session-recovery transcript rendering)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83157
- Related #80934 (made reseed fire by default for Claude CLI; this surface only became user-reachable after that landed)
- Related #79764 (introduced the reseed opt-in path; same surface)
- Related #79713 (predecessor — `loadCliSessionReseedMessages` returning `[]`; orthogonal)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External-contributor real-environment proof, captured on macOS 15.4 / Node 22 / `2026.5.12` brew install of OpenClaw with the Telegram channel + Claude CLI backend.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** when a long-running Claude-CLI session reseeds (e.g. `session_expired` retry), the rendered transcript exceeds the 12288-char cap, and the prefix slice keeps only the **oldest** turns. The most recent assistant reply and most recent user ask are silently dropped, and the agent's first response after recovery acts on a stale view of the conversation.

- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw `2026.5.12` brew-installed; Anthropic claude-cli backend over the official Telegram channel; multi-turn conversation reseeded after a session-expired error. Local mirror of the same defect reproduced with a unit-level fixture (>16k chars rendered history) — see Regression Test Plan section. Source-level fix verified identical to the prod-bundle patch already running on this machine via the local `openclaw-reseed-tail-slice-patch.py` mitigation script (script's `UPSTREAM_FIX_FINGERPRINT = "renderedHistoryRaw.slice(-maxHistoryChars)"` matches this PR's source).

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git checkout fix/cli-runner/reseed-tail-slice`
  2. `pnpm install` (already up-to-date)
  3. `pnpm build` — produced `dist/session-history-ad5FiFRu.js`
  4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts` — all 36 tests pass across the two project lanes including the three new regressions
  5. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` — all 82 tests pass across the cli-runner test surface
  6. `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — both clean
  7. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.ts src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts` — clean
  8. `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.ts src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
  9. Local patch script self-suppression check against the freshly-built dist — exit `0`, message `upstream reseed-tail-slice fix detected in session-history-ad5FiFRu.js (found 'renderedHistoryRaw.slice(-maxHistoryChars)'); no patching needed.`

- **Evidence after fix (real session capture, redacted):**

  Recovery preamble captured from a real Telegram → Claude-CLI reseed before this fix landed (this is what the agent was handed as `<conversation_history>` after an upstream session-expired retry). `[REDACTED]` blocks replace identifiable user-content text; the `[OpenClaw reseed history truncated]` marker and the mid-word cut-off `Th` immediately preceding it are the load-bearing details:

  ```
  Continue this conversation using the OpenClaw transcript below as prior session history.
  Treat it as authoritative context for this fresh CLI session.

  <conversation_history>
  Assistant: Yeah I'm here. I don't have context from a prior session — each conversation starts fresh. [REDACTED first turn ~250 chars]

  A: [REDACTED earliest user turn ~300 chars]

  S [REDACTED 12500+ chars of OLDEST turns kept by the buggy prefix slice]
  ... [REDACTED middle turns] ...
  Th
  [OpenClaw reseed history truncated]
  </conversation_history>

  <next_user_message>
  [REDACTED metadata block]

  You can reclaim it for me and then restart the gateway
  </next_user_message>
  ```

  Total rendered history length: **13568 chars** (cap is 12288). The slice kept the first 12288 chars — i.e. the OLDEST turns — and cut the latest assistant reply mid-word `Th[at one ...` immediately before the truncation marker. The user's `<next_user_message>` ("You can reclaim it for me and then restart the gateway") therefore arrives without the context it directly references — the most recent assistant turn was the very thing the user was responding to.

  Captured at `2026-05-17T03:57:21.779Z` (UTC).

  After this fix, the same input produces:

  ```
  <conversation_history>
  [OpenClaw reseed history truncated; older turns dropped]
  [REDACTED middle turns ...]
  Assistant: [REDACTED most recent assistant reply IN FULL — no mid-word cutoff]

  User: [REDACTED most recent user ask preceding the reseed]
  </conversation_history>
  ```

  The latest turn is preserved end-to-end; the marker leads, correctly describing that older turns were dropped.

- **Test runs after fix:**

  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts
   RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/adele_with_a_b/workplace/oss/openclaw

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  36 passed (36)
     Duration  ~900ms

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts
   Test Files  4 passed (4)
        Tests  82 passed (82)
     Duration  ~3.7s

  $ pnpm tsgo:core      # exit 0 (silent)
  $ pnpm tsgo:core:test # exit 0 (silent)
  $ pnpm build          # green; dist/session-history-ad5FiFRu.js produced
  ```

- **Local mitigation patch self-suppression check:**

  ```
  $ python3 -c "<simulate openclaw-reseed-tail-slice-patch.py against the local dist>"
  upstream reseed-tail-slice fix detected in session-history-ad5FiFRu.js (found 'renderedHistoryRaw.slice(-maxHistoryChars)'); no patching needed.
  exit=0
  ```

  This is the cleanest acceptance test that the source change is patch-equivalent — the local mitigation script that's been running on this machine since the defect was found will now silently no-op against any release containing this fix.

- **Observed result after fix:** the rendered prompt always contains the latest message in `params.messages`. The marker text `[OpenClaw reseed history truncated; older turns dropped]` accurately describes what was discarded (the prefix), not what was kept.

- **What was not tested:** a live OC instance running the freshly-built bundle in production-shape Telegram → Claude-CLI flow with a long enough conversation to trip the cap. The unit-level reproducer + freshly-built-dist patch-equivalence check together cover the same surface as the local mitigation script that has been running this exact transformation in production on M5 since the defect was first reproduced.

- **Before evidence:** the redacted recovery preamble above, captured from a real `~/.claude/projects/.../*.jsonl` thread on `2026-05-17T03:57:21.779Z`.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `params.messages` is in chronological order (oldest → newest), and the original `renderedHistoryRaw.slice(0, maxHistoryChars)` keeps the prefix and drops the suffix when over the cap. That is the opposite of what a session-recovery feature wants — you need the recent tail, not the ancient head. The trailing `[OpenClaw reseed history truncated]` marker reinforced the wrong mental model and made the bug hard to spot in code review.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** the existing `buildCliSessionHistoryPrompt` test in `session-history.test.ts` only asserted that the marker appears and that very-old content is absent when capped — it never asserted that the **latest** message in the input survives the rendering, which is the load-bearing property of a recovery feature. This PR adds that assertion.
- **Contributing context (if known):** the reseed path was opt-in until #79764 introduced the flag, and only became default-on for Claude CLI in #80934 (`2026.5.12`). Until then this defect was effectively unreachable, so it never surfaced under earlier tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:**
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file:** `src/agents/cli-runner/session-history.test.ts` — added three `it(...)` cases inside the existing `describe("buildCliSessionHistoryPrompt", ...)` block:
  1. `it("keeps the most recent turns when rendered history exceeds the cap", ...)` — locks in tail-slice semantics for the dominant uncompacted-reseed path.
  2. `it("preserves the compaction summary when the post-summary transcript exceeds the cap", ...)` — locks in structure-aware truncation: the leading `compactionSummary` entry is pinned as a prefix and the post-summary tail is sliced against the remaining budget.
  3. `it("caps oversize compaction summary; drops post-summary tail when summary alone exceeds the budget", ...)` — guards against the `slice(-0) === full tail` JS quirk when an oversize summary consumes the full `maxHistoryChars` budget.
- **Scenario the tests lock in:** given N messages whose joined rendered size exceeds `maxHistoryChars`, the LAST message's content MUST appear in the rendered prompt; the head-most content MUST NOT appear; the lead truncation marker MUST be present; a leading `compactionSummary` entry MUST survive even when the post-summary tail alone exceeds the cap; and an oversize summary MUST NOT cause a `slice(-0)` regression that leaks the entire tail.
- **Why these are the smallest reliable guardrails:** the bugs are fully observable at the pure-function level — no I/O, no fixtures beyond the messages array. Each test fails on the regression it covers (head-slice would keep the 8000-char `x` block; a blind tail-slice would drop the summary; a missing `slice(-0)` guard would leak the full tail) and passes on the fixed code.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** the existing `it("caps rendered reseed history before adding the next user message", ...)` only asserted the marker and absent-old-content, not the present-new-content property — that gap is what allowed this bug to land.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Reseeded CLI sessions no longer mysteriously act as if their last turn never happened when the rendered transcript exceeds 12288 chars.
- The truncation marker text changes from `[OpenClaw reseed history truncated]` (trailing) to `[OpenClaw reseed history truncated; older turns dropped]` (leading). The marker is internal to the system-injected `<conversation_history>` block consumed by the model, not user-facing chat text. No external caller is known to grep for the literal old marker; the existing test was the only matchable consumer and is updated in this PR.

## Implementation notes

- When `params.messages[0]` is a `compactionSummary` entry, render it as a pinned prefix and tail-slice only the post-summary transcript against `maxHistoryChars - summaryLength`.
- When the summary alone exceeds the cap, head-slice the summary itself so the prompt always honors `maxHistoryChars`; the post-summary tail is dropped in that case.
- Regression tests cover: recent-tail preservation, compaction-summary preservation when the post-summary tail exceeds the cap, and the oversize-summary case.

## Diagram

```
BEFORE (buggy: prefix slice — keeps OLDEST, drops LATEST)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [oldest turn 1] [turn 2] [turn 3] ... [recent] │  CUT  [latest]  │
│ ◄──────── KEPT (slice(0, maxHistoryChars)) ───►│ DROPPED         │
│                                                                   │
│  + trailing marker "[OpenClaw reseed history truncated]"         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    Symptom: agent ignores latest turn

AFTER (fixed: suffix slice — keeps LATEST, drops OLDEST)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [oldest turn 1] [turn 2] ...  CUT  │ [recent] [latest]            │
│  DROPPED                            │ ◄── KEPT (slice(-N)) ────►  │
│                                                                   │
│ leading marker "[OpenClaw reseed history truncated; older         │
│ turns dropped]" describes what was discarded ───────►            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    Outcome: latest turn always preserved
```

target.title: fix(google): remove outdated gemini-3.1-flash-lite preview-normalization
target.body:
## Summary
This pull request removes the outdated model-id normalization mapping that rewrote `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` to `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`. Since the Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite model has transitioned to General Availability (GA), it no longer requires the `-preview` suffix. Retaining this obsolete normalization caused user configuration files (`openclaw.json`) to be unexpectedly overwritten and displayed the preview identifier in the Web UI.

## Verification

### Behavior addressed
User's `openclaw.json` config file would get overwritten with `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` when saving/restoring, and the Web UI displayed the older preview ID instead of the GA ID.

### Real environment tested
Linux (Ubuntu 24.04 x64), Node v22.22.3.

### Exact steps or command run after this patch
1. Ran `pnpm test extensions/google/model-id.test.ts` to verify the updated mapping logic behavior.
2. Ran `pnpm test src/agents/model-selection.test.ts` to confirm model-selection behaviors resolve correctly to the GA identifier.
3. Ran `pnpm check:test-types` to ensure strict type check alignment across tests and core code.

### Evidence after fix
Tests passed with 100% success rate:
- `extensions/google/model-id.test.ts` -> 11/11 tests passed.
- `src/agents/model-selection.test.ts` -> 101/101 tests passed.

### Observed result after fix
- `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` now resolves natively as `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` without being modified or overwritten.

### What was not tested
Testing with live Google/Gemini and Vertex AI API calls (logic-only validation performed via extensive unit tests).
